name: Business Email Compromise (BEC)
severity: High to Critical (due to financial and reputational risk)
detection: Email gateway, SIEM, EDR, user report, cloud email audit logs
scenario: An attacker gains access to or spoofs a legitimate business email account to deceive internal staff, customers or partners into making unauthorised wire transfers, sharing credentials or altering financial records. This may involve phishing, credential theft or abuse of trusted relationships.
tools: SIEM (e.g., Sentinel, Splunk, QRadar); Email security gateways (e.g., Proofpoint, Mimecast, Microsoft Defender for Office; Cloud audit logs (Microsoft 365 Unified Audit Log, Google Workspace Admin; Console); Identity platforms (e.g., Okta, Azure AD, Duo); Threat intel feeds for spoofed domain detect

detSteps.title: Identify suspicious email account activity
detSteps.detail: Look for unusual login locations, new inbox rules created, unexpected email content sent from the account, or new OAuth app grants. Impossible travel (two logins from distant geographies within minutes) is a strong indicator of credential theft.

queries.splunk: index=cloud sourcetype=o365 OR sourcetype=gws | search action IN ("New-InboxRule","Add-MailboxPermission","Set-Mailbox") | table _time, user, action, params | sort _time
queries.sigma:
title: Business Email Compromise  BEC — Phishing Office Macro Spawn
id: 3f0b3182-38b8-5a46-a6eb-240023c265b4
status: stable
description: Detects Office application spawning shell — phishing payload execution.
tags:
    - attack.initial-access
    - attack.t1566
logsource:
    category: process_creation
    product: windows
detection:
    selection:
        ParentImage|endswith:
            - '\\winword.exe'
            - '\\excel.exe'
            - '\\outlook.exe'
        Image|endswith:
            - '\\powershell.exe'
            - '\\cmd.exe'
            - '\\wscript.exe'
    condition: selection
falsepositives:
    - Legitimate macro-enabled templates
date: 2026-07-01
level: high
queries.velociraptor: SELECT Pid, Name, CommandLine, Exe, CreateTime FROM pslist() ORDER BY CreateTime DESC LIMIT 150
queries.security_onion:
# Security Onion KQL — Business Email Compromise (BEC) (step 1)
# BEC / phishing email indicators
# Mailbox rule creation (forwarding — requires O365/EWS log ingest)
event.action:("Add-MailboxPermission" OR "New-InboxRule" OR "Set-InboxRule")
  AND (winlog.event_data.Parameters:*ForwardTo* OR winlog.event_data.Parameters:*DeliverToMailbox*)

# Office application spawning shell (phishing payload)
event.code:1
  AND process.parent.name:(winword.exe OR excel.exe OR powerpnt.exe OR outlook.exe)
  AND process.name:(powershell.exe OR cmd.exe OR wscript.exe OR mshta.exe)

# Suricata phishing signature
event.dataset:suricata.eve AND event.kind:alert
  AND (suricata.alert.signature:*phish* OR suricata.alert.category:"Attempted User Privilege Gain")
queries.osquery:
-- OSQuery: Business Email Compromise (BEC) (step 1)
-- Email client processes and suspicious child processes
SELECT p.pid, p.name, p.cmdline, pp.name AS parent
FROM processes p LEFT JOIN processes pp ON p.parent = pp.pid
WHERE pp.name IN ('outlook.exe','thunderbird.exe','teams.exe')
  AND p.name IN ('powershell.exe','cmd.exe','wscript.exe','mshta.exe');

-- Current authenticated user sessions
SELECT * FROM logged_in_users;

detSteps.title: Analyse email headers and sending infrastructure
detSteps.detail: Verify the sending domain, IP reputation, Reply-To address, and DKIM/DMARC/SPF results. Spoofed BEC emails often have a legitimate-looking From address but a different Reply-To, or originate from a domain with missing or failing authentication records.

queries.kql:
EmailEvents
| where TimeGenerated > ago(24h)
| where SenderFromAddress =~ '<suspect_address>'
| project TimeGenerated, SenderFromAddress, RecipientEmailAddress, Subject, SenderIPv4, AuthenticationDetails
| order by TimeGenerated desc
queries.qradar: SELECT "Sender" as sender, "Recipient" as recipient, "Subject" as subject, sourceip, starttime FROM events WHERE logsourcetypename(devicetype) ILIKE '%Office 365%' AND QIDNAME(qid) ILIKE '%email%' ORDER BY starttime DESC LAST 24 HOURS
queries.sigma:
title: Business Email Compromise  BEC — Phishing Office Macro Spawn
id: b3da3ae9-d513-506f-8051-6bac603615a8
status: stable

            - '\\wscript.exe'
    condition: selection

# Security Onion KQL — Business Email Compromise (BEC) (step 2)
# BEC / phishing email indicators
# Mailbox rule creation (forwarding — requires O365/EWS log ingest)
event.action:("Add-MailboxPermission" OR "New-InboxRule" OR "Set-InboxRule")

# Office application spawning shell (phishing payload)
event.code:1
  AND process.parent.name:(winword.exe OR excel.exe OR powerpnt.exe OR outlook.exe)

queries.osquery:
-- OSQuery: Business Email Compromise (BEC) (step 2)
-- Email client processes and suspicious child processes
SELECT p.pid, p.name, p.cmdline, pp.name AS parent
FROM processes p LEFT JOIN processes pp ON p.parent = pp.pid
WHERE pp.name IN ('outlook.exe','thunderbird.exe','teams.exe')
  AND p.name IN ('powershell.exe','cmd.exe','wscript.exe','mshta.exe');

-- Current authenticated user sessions
SELECT * FROM logged_in_users;

detSteps.title: Check for auto-forwarding rules and mailbox delegation
detSteps.detail: Attackers immediately set up auto-forwarding to an external address after gaining access, allowing persistent email monitoring even after the password is reset. Also check for mailbox delegation granting another account send-as rights.

queries.splunk: index=cloud sourcetype=o365 Operation=New-InboxRule | table _time, user, ClientIPAddress, Parameters | sort _time
queries.kql:
OfficeActivity
| where TimeGenerated > ago(24h)
| where Operation in ('New-InboxRule','Set-InboxRule','Set-Mailbox')
| where Parameters has_any ('ForwardTo','RedirectTo','ForwardingSmtpAddress')
| project TimeGenerated, UserId, ClientIPAddress, Operation, Parameters
| order by TimeGenerated desc
queries.qradar: SELECT username, QIDNAME(qid) as event, "Parameters" as params, ClientIPAddress, starttime FROM events WHERE logsourcetypename(devicetype) ILIKE '%Office 365%' AND QIDNAME(qid) ILIKE '%InboxRule%' ORDER BY starttime DESC LAST 24 HOURS
queries.sigma:
title: Business Email Compromise  BEC — Phishing Office Macro Spawn
id: 5a970e00-fd96-5e89-b76c-6a67a91d35c7

# Security Onion KQL — Business Email Compromise (BEC) (step 3)
# BEC / phishing email indicators
# Mailbox rule creation (forwarding — requires O365/EWS log ingest)
event.action:("Add-MailboxPermission" OR "New-InboxRule" OR "Set-InboxRule")
  AND (winlog.event_data.Parameters:*ForwardTo* OR winlog.event_data.Parameters:*DeliverToMailbox*)

# Office application spawning shell (phishing payload)

  AND process.parent.name:(winword.exe OR excel.exe OR powerpnt.exe OR outlook.exe)

-- Email client processes and suspicious child processes
SELECT p.pid, p.name, p.cmdline, pp.name AS parent
FROM processes p LEFT JOIN processes pp ON p.parent = pp.pid
WHERE pp.name IN ('outlook.exe','thunderbird.exe','teams.exe')
  AND p.name IN ('powershell.exe','cmd.exe','wscript.exe','mshta.exe');

-- Current authenticated user sessions
SELECT * FROM logged_in_users;

detSteps.title: Determine if finance, HR, or executives were engaged
detSteps.detail: BEC's primary goal is financial fraud or credential harvesting. Determine if the compromised account sent emails requesting wire transfers, invoice changes, W-2/payroll data, or credential resets to finance, HR, or vendor contacts.

queries.splunk: index=email src_user=[compromised_user] dest_user IN ([finance_team],[hr_team]) | table _time, subject, attachment | sort _time
queries.kql:
EmailEvents
| where TimeGenerated > ago(24h)
| where SenderFromAddress =~ '<compromised_user>'
| where RecipientEmailAddress has_any ('finance','payroll','accounts','hr','vendor')
| project TimeGenerated, SenderFromAddress, RecipientEmailAddress, Subject
| order by TimeGenerated desc
queries.qradar: SELECT "Sender" as sender, "Recipient" as recipient, "Subject" as subject, starttime FROM events WHERE logsourcetypename(devicetype) ILIKE '%Office 365%' AND "Sender" = '<compromised_user>' ORDER BY starttime DESC LAST 24 HOURS
queries.sigma:
title: Business Email Compromise  BEC — Phishing Office Macro Spawn
id: c791d0d1-a1c7-529f-a789-4ae532fd3925

detection:
    selection:

            - '\\excel.exe'
            - '\\outlook.exe'
        Image|endswith:
            - '\\powershell.exe'
            - '\\cmd.exe'
            - '\\wscript.exe'
    condition: selection

    - Legitimate macro-enabled templates
date: 2026-07-01

# BEC / phishing email indicators
# Mailbox rule creation (forwarding — requires O365/EWS log ingest)
event.action:("Add-MailboxPermission" OR "New-InboxRule" OR "Set-InboxRule")
  AND (winlog.event_data.Parameters:*ForwardTo* OR winlog.event_data.Parameters:*DeliverToMailbox*)

# Office application spawning shell (phishing payload)
event.code:1
  AND process.parent.name:(winword.exe OR excel.exe OR powerpnt.exe OR outlook.exe)
  AND process.name:(powershell.exe OR cmd.exe OR wscript.exe OR mshta.exe)

  AND (suricata.alert.signature:*phish* OR suricata.alert.category:"Attempted User Privilege Gain")

-- OSQuery: Business Email Compromise (BEC) (step 4)
-- Email client processes and suspicious child processes
SELECT p.pid, p.name, p.cmdline, pp.name AS parent
FROM processes p LEFT JOIN processes pp ON p.parent = pp.pid
WHERE pp.name IN ('outlook.exe','thunderbird.exe','teams.exe')
  AND p.name IN ('powershell.exe','cmd.exe','wscript.exe','mshta.exe');

-- Current authenticated user sessions
SELECT * FROM logged_in_users;

contSteps.title: Reset credentials and revoke all active sessions
contSteps.detail: Force a password reset through an admin-initiated flow. Revoke all active sessions, tokens, and refresh tokens for the account. If attacker controls the recovery email, change that too. Notify the user via an out-of-band channel (phone).

queries.kql:
SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(24h)
| where UserPrincipalName =~ '<compromised_user>'
| project TimeGenerated, UserPrincipalName, IPAddress, AppDisplayName, ResultType, LocationDetails
| order by TimeGenerated desc
queries.qradar: SELECT username, sourceip, QIDNAME(qid) as event, starttime FROM events WHERE username = '<compromised_user>' AND logsourcetypename(devicetype) ILIKE '%Azure AD%' ORDER BY starttime DESC LAST 24 HOURS

contSteps.title: Remove all malicious inbox rules immediately
contSteps.detail: Delete any auto-forwarding rules, deletion filters, or read-and-mark rules created by the attacker. In M365 use: Get-InboxRule -Mailbox [user] | Remove-InboxRule. In Google Workspace: check Filters and Forwarding settings in admin console.

queries.kql:
OfficeActivity
| where TimeGenerated > ago(24h)
| where Operation in ('New-InboxRule','Remove-InboxRule','Set-InboxRule')
| where UserId =~ '<compromised_user>'
| project TimeGenerated, UserId, Operation, Parameters, ClientIPAddress
| order by TimeGenerated desc
queries.qradar: SELECT username, QIDNAME(qid) as event, "Parameters" as params, starttime FROM events WHERE logsourcetypename(devicetype) ILIKE '%Office 365%' AND QIDNAME(qid) ILIKE '%InboxRule%' AND username = '<compromised_user>' ORDER BY starttime DESC LAST 24 HOURS

contSteps.title: Alert and work with finance to recall or block any pending transactions
contSteps.detail: If a fraudulent wire transfer or invoice change request was sent, immediately notify finance to freeze or recall the transaction. Time is critical — contact the destination bank within hours for any chance of recovery.

queries.kql:
EmailEvents
| where TimeGenerated > ago(48h)
| where SenderFromAddress =~ '<compromised_user>'
| where Subject has_any ('wire','transfer','invoice','payment','urgent')
| project TimeGenerated, SenderFromAddress, RecipientEmailAddress, Subject
| order by TimeGenerated desc
queries.qradar: SELECT "Sender" as sender, "Recipient" as recipient, "Subject" as subject, starttime FROM events WHERE logsourcetypename(devicetype) ILIKE '%Office 365%' AND "Sender" = '<compromised_user>' AND LOWER("Subject") LIKE '%wire%' OR LOWER("Subject") LIKE '%invoice%' ORDER BY starttime DESC LAST 48 HOURS

eradSteps.title: Fully audit the compromised mailbox
eradSteps.detail: Review the full sent folder, deleted items, calendar, and contacts for the attack window. Document all emails sent, received, and deleted by the attacker. Check for any data exfiltrated through email attachments.

queries.splunk: index=cloud sourcetype=o365 user=[compromised_user] | stats count by Operation | sort -count
queries.kql:
OfficeActivity
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where UserId =~ '<compromised_user>'
| summarize count() by Operation
| order by count_ desc
queries.qradar: SELECT username, QIDNAME(qid) as event, COUNT(*) as occurrences FROM events WHERE username = '<compromised_user>' AND logsourcetypename(devicetype) ILIKE '%Office 365%' GROUP BY username, event ORDER BY occurrences DESC LAST 7 DAYS
queries.velociraptor: SELECT Pid, Name, CommandLine, Exe, CreateTime FROM pslist() ORDER BY CreateTime DESC LIMIT 150

eradSteps.title: Enforce MFA and conditional access policies
eradSteps.detail: If MFA was not enabled, enforce it immediately. If MFA was bypassed (via phishing for the OTP or SIM-swap), investigate the bypass method and apply stronger controls (hardware key, number matching, phishing-resistant FIDO2).

queries.kql:
AuditLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(24h)
| where OperationName in ('User registered security info','MFA method enrolled')
| project TimeGenerated, OperationName, InitiatedBy, TargetResources
| order by TimeGenerated desc
queries.qradar: SELECT username, QIDNAME(qid) as event, starttime FROM events WHERE logsourcetypename(devicetype) ILIKE '%Azure AD%' AND (QIDNAME(qid) ILIKE '%MFA%' OR QIDNAME(qid) ILIKE '%security info%') ORDER BY starttime DESC LAST 24 HOURS

recSteps.title: Monitor the account for 30 days with enhanced alerting
recSteps.detail: Set alerts for any new inbox rules, unusual login locations, bulk email activity, or new OAuth grants for 30 days post-incident. Treat any anomaly as a priority re-investigation.

queries.splunk: index=cloud user=[recovered_user] | timechart count span=1d by Operation
queries.kql:
OfficeActivity
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| where UserId =~ '<recovered_user>'
| where Operation in ('New-InboxRule','Set-InboxRule','Add-MailboxPermission','SendAs')
| project TimeGenerated, UserId, Operation, ClientIPAddress, Parameters
| order by TimeGenerated desc
queries.qradar: SELECT username, QIDNAME(qid) as event, COUNT(*) as occurrences, starttime FROM events WHERE username = '<recovered_user>' AND logsourcetypename(devicetype) ILIKE '%Office 365%' GROUP BY username, event, starttime ORDER BY occurrences DESC LAST 30 DAYS
queries.velociraptor: SELECT Pid, Name, CommandLine, Exe, CreateTime FROM pslist() ORDER BY CreateTime DESC LIMIT 150

metrics.name: Detection Time
metrics.target: <15 minutes from phishing or suspicious email

metrics.name: Awareness Campaign
metrics.target: Stop wire transfer or mitigate within 24 hours

metrics.name: Post-Incident Monitoring Period
metrics.target: Minimum of 30 days for affected accounts
